title: Protocol for Spectral Digitization of Herbarium Specimens
author: IHerbSpec
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Official release of the  Protocol for the Spectral Digitization of Herbarium Specimens , authored by the International Herbarium Spectral Digitization Working Group (IHerbSpec).  
 This Zenodo record serves as the archival home for versioned releases of the IHerbSpec Protocol. All releases share a single concept DOI (DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18451589), to be used for citation and long-term discoverability as the protocol evolves. 
  Cite the protocol like this:  IHerbSpec. 2026. Protocol for the Spectral Digitization of Herbarium Specimens (v1.2). Zenodo.  https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18451589  
 The IHerbSpec Protocol is maintained as a  living standard  on the project website  (https://iherbspec.github.io) , which represents the most current, actively developed version. The website is generated using Quarto from openly maintained source code hosted in the canonical GitHub repository:  https://github.com/IHerbSpec/iherbspec.github.io . 
 Accordingly, this Zenodo archive includes: 
 
 
 the  full source code  and image files used to generate the IHerbSpec website and protocol, 
 
 
  rendered website snapshots  (*html files), and 
 
 
 a  compiled PDF  of the protocol, rendered from the same source as a single, unified document for offline use, printing, and long-term reference. 
 
 
 Each Zenodo release corresponds exactly to a tagged version of the GitHub repository and provides another version-specific DOI, while the concept DOI remains stable across all releases and should be used for citation. This structure supports transparent versioning, reproducibility, and reliable citation for both the current protocol and its historical versions. 
 All website and protocol code and content is licensed under a  Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0) . Users are free to share and adapt the material, provided attribution is given to  IHerbSpec Protocol (CC BY 4.0).  
  Abstract  
 Reflectance spectroscopy is a powerful, broadly integrative tool for capturing plant phenotypes. However, variation in instrumentation and measurement procedures introduces a high risk of data incompatibility and signal contamination across datasets. These challenges are amplified in herbarium specimens, which are subject to complex variation arising from specimen age, preservation techniques, and mounting practices. 
 To support global data harmonization and scientific reproducibility, this document presents a standardized, interoperable protocol for the spectral measurement of herbarium tissues. The protocol provides data-driven justification and community-informed guidance for measurement procedures and metadata fields, with the goal of improving data quality and enabling confident data aggregation across institutions.

 This Zenodo record serves as the archival home for versioned releases of the IHerbSpec Protocol. All releases share a single concept DOI (DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18451589), to be used for citation and long-term discoverability as the protocol evolves. 
  Cite the protocol like this:  IHerbSpec. 2026. Protocol for the Spectral Digitization of Herbarium Specimens (v1.2). Zenodo.  https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18451589  
 The IHerbSpec Protocol is maintained as a  living standard  on the project website  (https://iherbspec.github.io) , which represents the most current, actively developed version. The website is generated using Quarto from openly maintained source code hosted in the canonical GitHub repository:  https://github.com/IHerbSpec/iherbspec.github.io . 
 Accordingly, this Zenodo archive includes: 
 
 
 the  full source code  and image files used to generate the IHerbSpec website and protocol, 
 
 
  rendered website snapshots  (*html files), and 
 
 
 a  compiled PDF  of the protocol, rendered from the same source as a single, unified document for offline use, printing, and long-term reference. 
 
 
 Each Zenodo release corresponds exactly to a tagged version of the GitHub repository and provides another version-specific DOI, while the concept DOI remains stable across all releases and should be used for citation. This structure supports transparent versioning, reproducibility, and reliable citation for both the current protocol and its historical versions. 
 All website and protocol code and content is licensed under a  Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0) . Users are free to share and adapt the material, provided attribution is given to  IHerbSpec Protocol (CC BY 4.0).  
  Abstract  
 Reflectance spectroscopy is a powerful, broadly integrative tool for capturing plant phenotypes. However, variation in instrumentation and measurement procedures introduces a high risk of data incompatibility and signal contamination across datasets. These challenges are amplified in herbarium specimens, which are subject to complex variation arising from specimen age, preservation techniques, and mounting practices. 
 To support global data harmonization and scientific reproducibility, this document presents a standardized, interoperable protocol for the spectral measurement of herbarium tissues. The protocol provides data-driven justification and community-informed guidance for measurement procedures and metadata fields, with the goal of improving data quality and enabling confident data aggregation across institutions.
